corrected_fields.summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York published an Economic Policy Review containing four research articles on exchange rate dynamics and Treasury market structure, including historical analysis of the Treasury auction transition (1970-1975) and early examination of Treasury Inflation-Indexed Securities (TIIS) since their 1997 introduction.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: Historical reference document on Treasury market evolution and early TIIS performance. The auction institutionalization analysis (1970-1975) documents how Treasury successfully migrated to competitive bidding, a foundational structural change. TIIS analysis provides baseline expectations for inflation-linked securities market development.
corrected_fields.macro_context: The research examines exchange rate impacts on corporate profits and the relationship between speculator positioning and currency movements. No forward-looking policy implications for current markets.
corrected_fields.market_structure_context: Documents the structural evolution of Treasury auctions from fixed-price offerings to competitive bidding (1970-1975), establishing the framework still used today. Early TIIS market structure analysis shows liquidity and investor participation building over time.
